# @021.is/spine-env — LLM usage

> Type-safe zod-validated environment loader. Fails LOUDLY at module-load time when a variable is missing or invalid.

Install: bun add @021.is/spine-env

## Imports
- `import { defineEnv, common } from "@021.is/spine-env"` — single entry

## API
- `defineEnv(options)` — validate a source against per-key zod schemas; returns a frozen, typed object. Throws `Error` listing every bad key on failure. `DefineEnvOptions`: `schema` (`Record<string, ZodType>`), `source?` (default `process.env`), `strict?` (reject extras), `redactOnError?`
- `common` — ready-made schema builders: `nodeEnv()`, `httpsUrl()`, `url()`, `pgUrl()`, `bytes()`, `number()` (string→number), `boolean()` (`"true"|"false"|"1"|"0"`), `secret()` (non-empty)
- Type: `EnvOf<TSchema>`

## Example
```ts
import { defineEnv, common } from "@021.is/spine-env";
import { z } from "zod";

export const env = defineEnv({
  schema: {
    NODE_ENV: common.nodeEnv(),
    DATABASE_URL: common.pgUrl(),
    APP_URL: common.httpsUrl(),
    RESEND_API_KEY: common.secret(),
    PORT: common.number(),
    AUTH_CLIENT_ID: z.string().startsWith("app_"),
  },
});
// env.PORT is typed number, env.DATABASE_URL typed string; typos are compile errors.
```

## Notes
- Dep: `zod`. Server-only (reads `process.env`).
- Throws a plain `Error` (not a Spine exception) so it crashes the boot. Run the schema against `{}` to list required keys.
